mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and pushes equity prices higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising industrial production indicates resilient corporate capacity utilization, which offsets rate hike headwinds and supports positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises while FEDFUNDS is actively tightening, across a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals an approaching recession, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical GDP contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and rising inflation expectations forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/CPIAUCSL signal shows a positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns in more than half of all observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105.0, supply chain bottlenecks ease and capacity utilization normalizes, reducing input cost pressures and supporting positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 105.0 within a 2-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer confidence, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive RSAFS growth months are not significantly higher than baseline.
